Re: 
Our File No. A-85-l43 
Dear Victoria: 
Thank you for your letter of June 17, 1985, requesting con-
firmation of my advice concerning the lobbying disclosure reports 
filed by Robert E. Ross pursuant to the Political Reform Act 
(Gov. Code Sections 81000-91015). 
During our telephone conversa-
tion of June 5, 1985, you had asked whether Mr. Ross should amend 
his 1984 reports to reflect that he is employed by the lobbying 
firm o! Gilchrist-Ross Associates. 
You explained that although Mr. Ross is employed by Gilchrist-
Ross Associates, John Gilchrist was not actively associated with 
the firm during 1984 and upon resolution of some legal matters, 
Mr. Ross will be the sole proprietor of the firm. 
During 1984, 
Mr. Ross was the only lobbyist employed by the firm, and he filed 
Lobbyist Reports (Form 620) disclosing all of the payments received 
by Gilchrist-Ross Associates as if the payments were received by 
him directly. 
Each of the firm's clients filed a Report of Lobbyist 
Employer (Form 650) which also showed direct payments to Mr. Ross. 
Gilchrist-Ross Associates filed no employer reports for 1984. 
I advised you that because all of the payments received by 
the firm during 1984 were fully disclosed on Mr. Ross' Form 620, 
and because Commission regulations now allow a lobbyist who wholly 
owns a business entity and who is the only lobbyist employed by 
the entity to disclose all activities of the lobbyist and the 
entity on the Form 620 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section l86l4--copy 
enclosed), there is no need to file employer reports for Gilchrist-
Ross Associates for 1984 or to amend Mr. Ross' 1984 reports. 
However, I also advised you to review Mr. Ross' filings to ensure 
that all payments made by the firm were included as General 
Lobbying Expenses (Part III, Section B). 
If amendments are needed

for the purpose of including the firm's payments, you should also

Robert Ross as outlined in the enclosed FPPC fact sheet which

I also advised you to review 
the reports filed for 1983 to ensure that all activities were

Please let me know if I can be of further assistance in this

sections.question: Whether Robert E. Ross should amend his 1984 lobbying disclosure reports (Form 620) to reflect that he is employed by the lobbying firm of Gilchrist-Ross Associates, and whether the firm is required to file Lobbyist Employer Reports (Form 650) for 1984.
sections.conclusion: Because all of the payments received by the firm during 1984 were fully disclosed on Mr. Ross' Form 620, and because Commission regulations now allow a lobbyist who wholly owns a business entity and who is the only lobbyist employed by the entity to disclose all activities on the Form 620 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18614), there is no need to file employer reports for Gilchrist-Ross Associates for 1984 or to amend Mr. Ross' 1984 reports. However, Mr. Ross should review his filings to ensure all payments were included as General Lobbying Expenses, and if amendments are needed, should identify both Gilchrist-Ross Associates and Robert Ross.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a sole lobbyist working for a lobbying firm need to amend his 1984 disclosure reports to identify the firm employer, and must the firm file separate employer reports if all payments were already disclosed on the lobbyist's individual reports?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that no employer reports are required for Gilchrist-Ross Associates for 1984 and no amendments to Mr. Ross' reports are necessary under regulation Section 18614, which allows a sole-owner lobbyist to disclose all activities on his individual Form 620. However, Mr. Ross should verify all firm payments were properly disclosed as lobbying expenses and amend if needed to identify both the firm and himself.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two letters: incoming request from Victoria Cable dated June 17, 1985 (bottom) and FPPC response from Carla Wardlow dated July 3, 1985 (top). The FPPC response references a prior June 5 phone conversation. Both question and conclusion sections are present in the FPPC response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Robert E. Ross should amend his 1984 lobbying disclosure reports (Form 620) to reflect that he is employed by the lobbying firm of Gilchrist-Ross Associates, and whether the firm is required to file Lobbyist Employer Reports (Form 650) for 1984.

CONCLUSION: Because all of the payments received by the firm during 1984 were fully disclosed on Mr. Ross' Form 620, and because Commission regulations now allow a lobbyist who wholly owns a business entity and who is the only lobbyist employed by the entity to disclose all activities on the Form 620 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18614), there is no need to file employer reports for Gilchrist-Ross Associates for 1984 or to amend Mr. Ross' 1984 reports. However, Mr. Ross should review his filings to ensure all payments were included as General Lobbying Expenses, and if amendments are needed, should identify both Gilchrist-Ross Associates and Robert Ross.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms advice regarding lobbying disclosure requirements for Robert E. Ross and Gilchrist-Ross Associates for 1984, determining that no separate employer reports are required when a sole lobbyist fully discloses all firm payments on his individual disclosure forms.
